title: Create tracker UI runner skill and project workflow view
summary: Create a foundry skill that starts or opens the workflow tracker UI as a one-skill operation, and improve the tracker UI so operators can choose a project, inspect actual workflow tasks per project, and view linked artifacts without cramped wrapping.
acceptance_criteria: the skill has a lowercase hyphenated folder under .agents/skills with matching frontmatter name and Codex metadata
the skill defaults to fast open/start behavior: open the existing tracker server when running, otherwise start Astro and open the UI
the skill exposes an optional verification path for tests and builds without making verification mandatory for normal opening
the UI provides a project chooser rather than only an all-project/current-project toggle
the selected project view foregrounds workflow tasks and their status, Matt phase, next action, and useful workflow context
linked artifacts are truncated, summarized, or progressively disclosed so long paths do not create cramped table wrapping
each task exposes a safe continue action that generates the exact $continue-task command for copying
the UI remains read-only against project and task JSON
tests or generated-output checks prove the project filter and artifact display behavior
workflow state validation and tracker verification pass

context_snapshot.summary: Load workflow-foundry state, all non-done tasks, Matt/ECC references, the existing tracker UI task, and skill creation rules before shaping or implementing the tracker runner skill and project workflow view.

approved_artifacts.approval_note: Approved by user instruction to proceed from grilling through PRD, issues, and full implementation for the tracker UI runner skill.

session_log.event: Created intake task after user invoked initiate-task for a foundry skill that can run the tracker UI and requested a more intuitive per-project workflow view with non-cramped linked artifacts.
Continued task into grilling after loading workflow-foundry state, existing tracker UI implementation, linked artifacts, and Matt Pocock ask-matt/grilling references.

Resolved from repo inspection: the current Astro tracker already reads project/task JSON read-only, but the UI uses an all-project/current-project toggle and renders linked artifacts as a long joined string that causes cramped wrapping.

Working target: add a foundry skill for one-step tracker UI operation, replace the current-project-only toggle with a project chooser, and render linked artifacts through compact summaries or progressive disclosure.
Resolved runner behavior: workflow-tracker-ui should default to fast open/start, with optional verification available but not required for normal use.

Resolved Codex handoff boundary: task UI actions should generate a $continue-task command and may offer a best-effort app handoff only when a supported Codex app mechanism exists, rather than silently executing commands from static browser code.
Wrote PRD for the tracker UI runner skill and project workflow view; moved task to PRD phase with issues as the next action.
Moved task back to grilling at user request to further interrogate the tracker UI before issue breakdown or implementation.
Resolved primary UI layout: use a left project sidebar and a right task/workflow pane, so project switching stays visible while task lists and details have room.
Resolved task click behavior: selecting a task opens an inline detail panel first, with a clear Continue in Codex action inside the panel instead of immediately switching context.

Resolved phase display nuance: the UI should distinguish pre-Matt workflow state such as intake, project preload, and phase-guard readiness from the later Matt flow phases like grilling, PRD, issues, implement, and code-review.

Resolved lifecycle visualization: the right pane should include a lifecycle rail showing Intake/Preflight before Grilling, PRD, Issues, Implement, Code Review, and Done, with the selected task highlighted at its current point.
Resolved task list default: show active/open tasks as the primary list and keep completed tasks available in a collapsed Done section.

Resolved project sidebar content: each project entry should show project name, slug, active task count, blocked count, and the hottest/current phase while staying compact as navigation rather than a dashboard.

Resolved linked artifact presentation: group artifacts by type such as PRD, Issues, Skills, Tracker, Tests, and Other, but show human-readable titles or filenames first rather than raw paths, with restrained color cues for organization.
Resolved task row styling: use dense flat outlined rows with subtle fill, text-first content, and no shadow, bevel, or dimensional card treatment.
Resolved task detail panel behavior: use a right-side drawer inside the task pane on desktop and an expanded section under the selected task on mobile.
Removed direct Codex app handoff from current UI scope at user request; continue action should only generate/copy the exact $continue-task command for now.
Resolved continue command format: copied commands must be super explicit and always include both project:<slug> and task:<task-id>.
Resolved continue command UI: the detail panel should show the explicit $continue-task command in a monospace command box with a copy button.
Resolved session log display: show the latest 3-5 session events by default in the task detail panel, with an expandable control for the full log.
Resolved project sidebar search: include a compact text filter over project name and slug so the sidebar scales as more projects are added.
Resolved task pane search: include a selected-project-scoped task filter over task title, id, status, phase, and artifact title.
Resolved right-pane project summary: show a compact selected-project summary strip with Open, Blocked, In Review, Done, plus goal and domain.
Resolved theme behavior: dark mode remains the default tracker theme, with the existing light/dark toggle preserved.
Resolved UI persistence: remember the last selected project and task in localStorage as read-only browser UI state, without writing to workflow JSON.
Resolved lifecycle rail interaction: rail phase chips should be clickable filters scoped to the selected project, while still highlighting the selected task's current phase.
Resolved task row next-action display: show a one-line truncated next action in each task row, with the full next action available in the selected task detail panel.

Closed grilling at user direction, refreshed the PRD from all accepted design decisions, created four vertical-slice issue artifacts, and moved the task to implement with explicit approvals for the tracker UI, data tests, and workflow-tracker-ui skill files.
Implemented the v2 tracker console, derived workflow-state fields, generated-output coverage, and workflow-tracker-ui runner skill.
Verified with npm run verify, workflow-state validation, Chrome smoke check at http://127.0.0.1:4321, and saved review screenshot evidence.

Completed a focused code-review pass for this task's touched files with no blocking findings; kept the task in code-review while artifacts remain uncommitted so workflow validation can link changed files to a non-done task.
Closed during cleanup after tracker UI runner/project workflow view had already completed code review, and the stale aggregate-count test was updated to derive counts from current JSON state.
